notes: This file is the manual evidence register for the public website release track.
Set status to passed only after visible or operator-verified release evidence exists.
Allowed statuses: pending, passed, failed, waived.

checks.title: The target system is explicitly decided as the public Odoo website frontend on VM220 and not a public Odoo admin path
checks.evidence: VM220 serves the Odoo website on / with title Home | FraWo; direct Host-header preview on 192.168.2.22 returns apex redirect and www homepage; direct global IPv6 HTTP preview on 2a00:1e:ef80:7c01:be24:11ff:feaa:bbcc also returns the FraWo homepage.

checks.title: The public Odoo-managed website content is the intended GbR release content
checks.evidence: Public hold mode is active on frawo-tech.de and www.frawo-tech.de. The half-finished Odoo public page was deliberately replaced by a neutral maintenance page until CI, design, and final release content are approved.

checks.title: Apex and www browser behavior are visibly verified for the website release scope
checks.evidence: Public hold mode is active. HTTP now serves a neutral maintenance page instead of the half-finished Odoo website. HTTPS is still not green. This is an intentional temporary public state, not a release-ready website acceptance.

checks.title: The public website visibly includes the intended radio presence or player path
checks.evidence: Public hold mode is active. The radio block is intentionally not exposed on the temporary maintenance page; visible public radio integration remains pending until the real website release resumes.

checks.title: Public release mail model for webmaster, franz, info and noreply is verified
checks.evidence: webmaster@frawo-tech.de and franz@frawo-tech.de are technically verified for IMAP and SMTP AUTH; public release decision for info@frawo-tech.de and noreply@frawo-tech.de is still open.

checks.title: The chosen public TLS automation path is configured and documented
checks.evidence: Caddy on VM220 is active on 80/443 and ACME now hits the cutover target, but certificate issuance still fails because the public IPv4 path 92.211.33.54:80/443 is not open. IPv6 HTTP is live; router forwarding for IPv4 remains the concrete blocker.

checks.title: SPF, DKIM and DMARC are all intentionally configured for public release
checks.evidence: MX and DMARC are visible in the live checks; SPF is currently not visible and DKIM is not yet documented as verified for release.

checks.title: Rollback for DNS, TLS and host-switch is complete and usable
